title: practical english usage - Part 60
content:
to act, recommended behaviour, permission, willingness and similar matters.

Most modal verbs can also express various ideas relating to obligation, freedom 2    obligation, freedom to act, etc (►   73-81) We may get a new car. It can't be true.

It will rain tomorrow. She should be here soon.

possible or impossible.

situations or events: for instance to say that something is certain, probable, Most modal verbs can be used to express degrees of certainty about facts, 1    degrees of certainty (►   69-72) can have very similar uses. Modal  meanings fall into four groups.

each modal verb has more than one use; on the other hand, different modals to happen, or which  have not happened. This is a complicated area of grammar: necessary, which  we  want to happen, which  we  are not sure about, which  tend about things which  we  expect, which  are or are not possible, which  we  think are particular events have definitely happened. We   use them, for example, to talk We   do not usually use modal verbs to say that situations definitely exist, or that meanings be able to, be supposed to, had better and need.

Some other verbs and expressions are used in similar ways to modals: have to, example, they have no infinitives, participles or past tenses . For details, ►    68.1.

might and must. Their grammar is different from that of other verbs: for The  modal auxiliary verbs are will, would, shall, should, can, could, ought, may, What    are   modal     auxiliary       verbs?

Section 7  Modal  Auxiliary Verbs grammar •  Section 7  Modal  Auxiliary Verbs For modal-like uses of need, ►    532.   For modal-like uses of dare,  ►    43 1.

For should in subordinate clauses (e.g. lt‘s funny she should say that), ►    232.5.

For polite uses of would in 'distancing’  structures, ►    3 1 1 .

For will as a future auxiliary, ►    38.   For would in conditional structures, ►    239, 241.
